growth_records.medium: Defined lactate/sulfate chemostat medium (full composition in refs 11 and 36; not restated here)

up.method: reported: reactor sulfate 0.5,1,2,5 mM; ~50% consumed; no volumetric uptake rate given

sec.units: fmol H2S per cell per day (±8.9)
sec.method: reported: low sulfate experiments (0.5-5 mM), rate held constant at 22 (±8.9) fmol H2S per cell per day for D. alaskensis

growth_records.cite: Bertran et al. Oxygen isotope effects during microbial sulfate reduction: applications to sediment cell abundances. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0618-2. [PMC7242377]
growth_records.snippet: For the low sulfate experiments (0.5–5 mM), rate was held constant at 15 (±5.3) and 22 (±8.9) fmol H2S per cell per day for D. vulgaris and D. alaskensis, respectively ... 34ε varied from 0.1 to 11.4‰ for D. alaskensis
growth_records.notes: Marine pure strain G-20, anaerobic chemostat, 24C, pH 7.00, low ambient sulfate 0.5-5 mM, csSRR held at 22±8.9 fmol H2S/cell/day (reported secretion/metabolic rate). No mu in h-1 and no per-gDW flux; growth physiology given only as cell-specific sulfate reduction (=H2S production) rate. Medium composition in cited refs 11/36 (medium_composition_stated=false).
